description_general: Production of Lindner steel pedestals and raw material extraction through manufacturing at Arnstorf plant.
description_a1: Raw material supply: galvanised steel( Steel sheets and rolled steel), Locking glue, thread sealant and EVA gaskets .
description_a2: Transport of raw materials to factory gate, including internal logistics. Based on typical distances and transport modes.
description_a3: Manufacturing at Lindner Arnstorf plant: stamping, forming, welding, galvanising. Powered by electricity and compressed air.

description_general: Transport of steel pedestals from factory gate to construction site.
description_s1: Transport distance 100 km by diesel truck.

description_general: Installation on site has low environmental impact. Only packaging disposal is accounted for.
description_s1: Packaging waste: Wooden pallets and cardboard can be reused or recycled. No significant energy or emissions from installation itself.

description_general: Deconstruction and dismantling at end of life.
description_s1: Trained personnel dismantle pedestals on site to ensure maximum recyclability. Steel components are separated for recycling. Low energy input due to modular and adjustable system design.

description_general: Transport from deconstruction site to recycling facility.
description_s1: Steel transported 50 km to nearest recycling facility by diesel truck.
95% of steel pedestal reaches end-of-waste status and is sent for recycling. Adhesive and plastic gaskets are incinerated (no recycling credit for these in Module D)
5% of non-recyclable material directed to landfill.

description_general: Reuse / recovery / recycling potential beyond system boundary.
description_s1: Credit from recycling of net scrap steel substituting primary steel production at an external facility. Negative value indicates a carbon benefit. Benefits reflect energy savings and reduced raw material consumption from high steel recyclability.
